stimulus: The following text is adapted from a fictional 1915 poem. It is autumn, glorious, fire-leafed autumn, The very breath of nature’s tired year When all her bursting life begins to fade. To me alone it is a time of rest, A void and quiet space between two seasons.
stem: As used in the text, what does the phrase “a void” most nearly mean?
rationale: Choice D is correct because "a void" means empty or unoccupied, matching the description of a quiet space between two seasons. Choice A is incorrect because uselessness isn’t implied. Choice B is incorrect because forgotten suggests memory, not emptiness. Choice C is incorrect because incomplete suggests something unfinished, not empty.
